cataloging_subject.label: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (1875 compilation)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The exact edition-level compilation represented by University of Michigan item AFY7823.0001.001: the title page and six editorial divisions on printed pages 1-240 of Carl Horstmann's 1875 Altenglische Legenden. The six divisions contain eight non-overlapping medieval intellectual texts represented through ten complete or deliberately incomplete printed witness streams, plus selected Patrick variants from two further witnesses and a comparison with a witness whose opening leaves are lost. This record does not collapse the compilation into one medieval work, count overlapping DIMEV record 5875 as a ninth text, transfer manuscript dates to the compilation, or identify the repository XML, U-M encoding, printed edition, manuscripts, and facsimiles as one object. Horstmann's forty-four-page German introduction is outside the encoded content unit except for its final errata page and is modeled as source evidence rather than as a ninth content item.

catalog_summary.title: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer

catalog_summary.region: England (manuscript and linguistic context; no single composition place established)

titles.value: Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer
notes: The punctuation is regularized from the title page; the four named groups encompass six editorial divisions and eight intellectual texts.

titles.value: Old English Legends: Childhood of Jesus; Nativity of Jesus; Barlaam and Josaphat; Saint Patrick’s Purgatory

assertion.notes: A discovery translation of the historical German title; 'Altenglische' here refers to medieval English, not specifically Old English language.

agents.name: Carl Horstmann
notes: Editor, compiler, transcriber, collator, and German commentator of the 1875 volume.

agents.name: Anonymous South English Legendary poets and revisers
notes: A collective discovery label, not a claim that one person composed every represented SEL text or version.

agents.name: Anonymous poet of Jesus and the Masters of the Law
Anonymous Vernon Barlaam and Josaphat poet
Anonymous expanded Northern Homily Cycle poet

assertion.notes: Horstmann assembled the six editorial divisions and their parallel witness streams as this 1875 volume.
The Patrick apparatus uses Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 selectively in addition to the three fully printed streams.
DIMEV describes the 1,854-line poem as translated from French; neither translator nor poet is identified.

notes: Publication date of the cataloged edition-level compilation, not a medieval composition date.

date_statements.display: Late thirteenth or early fourteenth century (Laud Misc. 108 target hand)

assertion.notes: Witness date only; it does not date either poem's composition.

date_statements.display: First quarter of the fourteenth century (Ashmole 43)

assertion.notes: The current public catalog does not support a narrower date; this is witness evidence for all three represented Egerton streams, not a shared composition date.

date_statements.display: Fourth quarter of the fourteenth to first quarter of the fifteenth century (Harley MS 4196)
Second quarter of the fifteenth century (Bodley 779)

places.label: England
notes: Discovery context for the represented Middle English manuscript traditions, not a precise place of composition for any of the eight texts.

scope.description: Manuscript and linguistic context of the represented medieval texts

assertion.notes: This supports a broad discovery region only and does not transfer any witness production place or dialect localization into a composition claim.

scope.description: Title, headings, arguments, notes, and other editorial framing
Scattered Latin rubrics, biblical quotations, and brief formulae embedded across the otherwise Middle English catalog unit

form_statements.description: All eight intellectual texts are verse; German prose headings, arguments, and notes are editorial paratext rather than prose content items.
Predominantly rhyming couplets or long-line couplets; DIMEV also records a terminal quatrain form for the Infancy poem.
Eight-line abababab stanzas, with a short irregular close in the printed representation.

subject_statements.label: Medieval Jewish-Christian polemic
notes: The poem deploys hostile medieval anti-Jewish stereotypes; this neutral subject description does not endorse them.

summaries.value: Horstmann's 1875 compilation prints Middle English verse on the childhood and nativity of Jesus, three distinct Barlaam and Josaphat poems, the South English Legendary life of Patrick with his Purgatory in three full versions, and a Vernon debate between Jesus and learned masters. It juxtaposes parallel manuscript streams and selected variants rather than presenting one medieval authorial collection.

Six editorial divisions contain eight intellectual texts: one Infancy poem; the Conception of Mary in Ashmole and Egerton versions followed by the distinct Expanded Nativity in Egerton; a Bodley Barlaam and Josaphat; the Patrick/Purgatory poem in Ashmole, Egerton, and Laud versions; Jesus and the Masters from Vernon; and two further, mutually distinct Barlaam and Josaphat poems from Vernon and Harley 4196.

DIMEV 5875 describes a passage beginning on Egerton f. 39v inside the 897-line Expanded Nativity and is therefore an overlapping repertory description, not a ninth non-overlapping item. DIMEV 4178 and 938, by contrast, meet at a real boundary after Egerton line 276 and are modeled as two intellectual texts despite Horstmann's continuous Zur Geburt Jesu division.

extent.description: Eight non-overlapping intellectual texts represented through fifteen structural records: six top-level editorial divisions, nested work boundaries, and version records. The XML contains 8,746 L elements in 111 LG elements; 8,744 are verbal verse lines, while one Vernon and one Harley dotted lacuna display each occupy an L row without verbal text. The 8,744 count is an edition-representation extent that includes parallel versions, not a claim that the eight abstract works total 8,744 unique canonical lines.

content_parts.label: I. Kindheit Jesu — The Apocryphal History of the Infancy
content_parts.summary: A Middle English infancy narrative follows Jesus, Mary, and Joseph through miracles in Egypt and episodes of the child's supernatural power; Horstmann prints the sole known Laud Misc. 108 witness.

content_parts.label: II. Zur Geburt Jesu
content_parts.summary: Horstmann interleaves Ashmole and Egerton versions of the Conception of Mary, then continues only the Egerton stream into the distinct Expanded Nativity.

locators.value: pp. 62-110; Ashmole and Egerton physical pages are serialized in XML reading order

content_parts.label: Conception of Mary
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary revision of the Marian section of DIMEV 6380, with an added prologue, represented by Ashmole and Egerton versions.

locators.value: pp. 64-81, parallel Ashmole and Egerton streams

notes: DIMEV describes a 280-line work; the encoded Horstmann streams contain 278 and 276 lines. The discrepancy is preserved rather than normalized.

content_parts.label: A. Ashmole 43 version
content_parts.summary: The 278-line Ashmole version, including its prologue and Marian narrative.

locators.value: ff. 208v-212r; prologue ff. 208v-209v, main narrative ff. 209v-212r

content_parts.label: B. Egerton 1993 version, lines 1-276
content_parts.summary: The opening 276 lines of Horstmann's continuous Egerton stream represent the Conception of Mary before the Expanded Nativity begins.
notes: DIMEV's active record gives ff. 27-30 for this component; that endpoint does not delimit the following Expanded Nativity, which continues in the same manuscript through f. 40.

content_parts.label: Expanded Nativity
content_parts.summary: A 897-line Egerton continuation narrates the Nativity and Christmas Gospel cycle, ending incomplete in the manuscript.
notes: DIMEV 5875 indexes an overlapping f. 39v passage inside this 897-line text; it is not counted separately.

content_parts.label: III. Barlaam und Josaphat — Bodley version
content_parts.summary: A South English Legendary Barlaam and Josaphat poem recounts Josaphat's conversion, instruction by Barlaam, trials, rule, renunciation, and sanctity; this version is distinct from both appendix versions.
notes: The current Bodleian catalog TEI and DIMEV v1.0 both say ff. 228v-302r. Direct inspection shows unrelated text on f. 228v/canvas 470, the exact Horstmann incipit beginning on f. 288v/canvas 596, and the explicit on f. 302r/canvas 623. The operative locator therefore preserves the visually verified correction while documenting the source error.

content_parts.label: IV. Das Fegfeuer des heiligen Patrick
content_parts.summary: The South English Legendary life of Patrick and account of his Purgatory follows his mission in Ireland, the divinely revealed penitential pit, a knight's journey through punishments and paradise, and exhortations to penance. Horstmann prints three full versions and selected collateral variants.

locators.value: pp. 149-211; full streams begin on pp. 151, 175, and 177

content_parts.label: a. Ashmole 43 version
content_parts.summary: Horstmann's 632-line base stream, with Cotton Julius D IX variants and selected Bodley 779 readings in the apparatus.

content_parts.label: b. Egerton 1993 version
content_parts.summary: A complete 711-line Egerton version printed after the Ashmole base stream.

content_parts.label: c. Laud Misc. 108 Purgatory version
content_parts.summary: A 673-line Laud version that omits the opening life of Patrick and begins directly with the Purgatory; its first two lines are an addition according to Horstmann's manuscript note.

content_parts.label: Beilage I. Jesus and the Masters of the Law
content_parts.summary: At age twelve Jesus debates learned Jewish masters in the Temple, answers their challenges through Christian typology, and is found by Mary. The poem contains hostile medieval anti-Jewish polemic and is described here historically rather than affirmatively.
notes: Horstmann's heading says f. 300; the current catalog, DIMEV, and direct images establish f. 301rc-vb.

content_parts.label: Beilage II. Two further Barlaam and Josaphat poems
content_parts.summary: Horstmann places two distinct Middle English Barlaam and Josaphat poems side by side: a Vernon poem with a missing leaf and a Northern Homily Cycle poem from Harley 4196 that ends incomplete.

content_parts.label: A. Vernon Barlaam and Josaphat
content_parts.summary: A distinct couplet version of Barlaam and Josaphat survives around the loss of Vernon f. 102; Horstmann estimates the missing leaf at roughly 480 verses.
notes: One dotted lacuna row at the break is not counted as a verbal verse line; Horstmann resumes numbering at 570 and ends at 792.

content_parts.label: B. Northern Homily Cycle Barlaam and Josaphat
content_parts.summary: A Northern Middle English couplet version from the expanded Northern Homily Cycle narrates Josaphat's conversion but breaks off at the bottom of the final surviving column.
notes: One final dotted/incomplete display row is excluded from the verbal-line count.

evidence.summary: Establishes six top-level editorial divisions, the nested version structure, 8,744 verbal verse lines plus two lacuna display rows, and a 284-image PB sequence. It separates Egerton lines 1-276 as DIMEV 4178 from lines 277-1173 as the exactly 897-line DIMEV 938.

evidence.citation: Carl Horstmann, ed., Altenglische Legenden: Kindheit Jesu; Geburt Jesu; Barlaam und Josaphat; St. Patrik’s Fegefeuer (Paderborn: Ferdinand Schöningh, 1875).
evidence.locator: title page; introduction pp. i-xliv; texts pp. 1-240
evidence.summary: Complete immediate edition supporting the compilation boundary, headings, page ranges, witness claims, transcription streams, selective Patrick apparatus, Vernon lost-leaf estimate, and incomplete Harley ending.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 2605, 4178, 938, 2965, 4742, 3120, 54, 58, and overlapping record 5875.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml and linked manuscript/bibliography records; README.md; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for eight non-overlapping work identities, IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, forms, subjects, direct and collateral witnesses, loci, extents, edition page ranges, and the overlapping status of record 5875.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscript catalog records for Laud Misc. 108, Ashmole 43, Bodley 779, and MS Eng. poet. a. 1 (Vernon).
evidence.summary: Official evidence for current shelfmarks, dates, support, extent, origin and dialect cautions, target loci, and the catalog's erroneous Bodley Barlaam start at f. 228v.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for direct inspection of four complete Digital Bodleian IIIF objects.
evidence.summary: Exact start and end canvases verify all Bodleian target portions, the Vernon f. 102 loss, the Jesus/Masters handoff, and the Bodley Barlaam correction from cataloged f. 228v to visually demonstrated f. 288v.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog records for Egerton MS 1993, Harley MS 4196, Cotton MS Julius D IX, and Harley MS 2277.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for current shelfmarks, broad manuscript dates, physical descriptions, collection contents, damage or incompleteness, and the absence of currently delivered legacy public images. DIMEV and Horstmann supply exact target loci where the holding records are coarse.

evidence.citation: General Corpus lineage manifest for afy7823.
evidence.summary: Binds the catalog unit and every content part to the edition, encoding, repository artifact, direct witnesses, collateral witnesses, facsimiles, access routes, rights records, and source evidence without conflating layers.

open_questions.question: What are the composition dates, places, and authorial or redaction histories of each of the eight medieval texts?
next_steps: Consult work-specific scholarship beyond DIMEV and Horstmann for each text.
Keep witness dates and dialect localizations separate from composition claims.
Do not promote Horstmann's historical reconstruction of South English Legendary development into exact work dates without modern support.

open_questions.question: Can complete current images of Egerton MS 1993 verify the exact Conception/Expanded Nativity boundary, Patrick locus, and end conditions directly?
next_steps: Request complete color photography of Egerton MS 1993, identifying ff. 27-40 and 119v-128 separately.
Compare every boundary against Horstmann, DIMEV, and the physical foliation.
Retain ff. 27-40 for the whole Zur Geburt Jesu stream and the line-276/277 intellectual boundary until direct images support a refinement.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian catalog and DIMEV correct the Barlaam and Josaphat start from f. 228v to the visually demonstrated f. 288v?
next_steps: Send Bodleian and DIMEV the canvas-470, canvas-596, and canvas-623 evidence with Horstmann's folio milestones.
Preserve both the claimed and corrected locators with provenance until the providers update their records.

open_questions.question: Which exact Cotton Julius D IX and Bodley 779 Patrick readings did Horstmann collate, and can every apparatus note be assigned to a current folio and line?
next_steps: Transcribe Horstmann's Patrick apparatus into a source-indexed collation table.
Obtain Cotton Julius D IX images and use the complete Bodley 779 facsimile to verify each selected reading.
Continue modeling Cotton and Bodley as selective collation witnesses, not full printed streams.

open_questions.question: Can the roughly 480 verses lost with Vernon f. 102 be reconstructed from an independent copy of DIMEV 54?
next_steps: Monitor DIMEV and new manuscript identifications for another witness.
Keep Horstmann's dotted lacuna and resumed lineation explicit rather than silently joining f. 101v to f. 103r.

open_questions.question: Can U-M supply the historical 284 linked TIFF files and production records for the AFY7823 OCR item?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for the original TIFF production set, OCR workflow records, and current delivery or reuse terms.
Preserve the PB REF versus SEQ transformation separately from the current unavailability of the images.

notes: The XML language header records only ger, but the inspected content is primarily Middle English verse with German editorial framing and brief Latin and Anglo-Norman embeddings.

The 284 PB elements have unique consecutive REF filenames 00000001.tif-00000284.tif and unique consecutive SEQ values 1-284. REF and SEQ diverge where Horstmann's Ashmole/Egerton and Egerton/Laud physical pages are serialized into electronic reading order; this is a carrier-order transformation, not duplicate-image evidence.
Target-scoped validation is required because this record deliberately models one edition-level compilation rather than a synthetic witness census for every included medieval text.
